mnemonics: Sample Size, Sample Mean, Sample SD, Population SD, Z-score
keyPoints: When comparing the means of two small samples, use the Test of Significance for means of two small samples.
Null hypothesis: μ1 = μ2 (The means of the two samples are equal).
Alternative hypothesis: μ1 ≠ μ2 (The means of the two samples are not equal).
Calculate the pooled sample standard deviation (s_p) as the square root of the pooled sample variance.
Calculate the Z-score as (x̄1 - x̄2) / sqrt(s_p^2 * (1/n1 + 1/n2))
Use a Z-table or calculator to find the critical Z-score for the desired significance level.
